phonetic: ﾉ厂翰埇ﾉ騨刃; ﾉ厂翰埇ﾃｦn
meaning: adj.髦ｿ譖ｼ(髦ｿ諡我ｼｯ荳懷漉驛ｨ豐ｿ豬ｷ蝨ｰ蛹ｺ)
examples: I believe that a modern reader cannot know the associations that a line of ancient oman poetry or prose had for any particular educated sixteenth-century reader.
But even Oman has been splashed.
Oman has an estimated population of 3 million.
Just an hour east of Abu Dhabi, Al Ain sits on the border with Oman.
Another alley sells honey from Yemen and dates from Qatar, Saudi Arabia and Oman .
